title: acmella ciliata
intro: Acmella ciliata, also known as the toothache plant, is a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, particularly the sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Acmella ciliata include reducing pain and inflammation, treating toothaches and gum infections, and alleviating digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas, tinctures, or infused oils, and can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
